config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan সমাজ, karena justru perlindungan terhadap orang per orang adalah dasar masyarakat yang adil. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa menghapus kebebasan, martabat, dan hak dasar setiap orang. Kalau prioritas selalu jatuh ke masyarakat, itu mudah disalahgunakan oleh pihak yang berkuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the statement as written, because in the United States I see individual rights as the default boundary that society should not cross lightly. Society does have legitimate interests in public safety, health, and fairness, but those interests need narrow limits and strong justification, or they become an excuse for government overreach. Agent A’s warning matters: once “the greater good” is used too loosely, it can be abused by people in power. I would say the better principle is that society can restrict individual rights only when the harm is real, specific, and clearly outweighed by the need.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju kalau kepentingan masyarakat ditempatkan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara minoritas, membungkam kritik, atau membenarkan keputusan yang tidak adil, jadi kekhawatiran soal penyalahgunaan itu sangat nyata. Kepentingan bersama memang penting untuk hal seperti kesehatan publik, ketertiban, dan bencana, tetapi pembatasan hak harus ketat, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan dijadikan alasan otomatis. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dilindungi dulu, lalu kepentingan bersama dicari tanpa menginjak martabat orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society's interests ahead of individual rights as a general rule, because in the United States that usually becomes a blank check for power. Public health, safety, and emergency response can justify limits, but only when they are narrow, temporary, and backed by real evidence rather than vague appeals to the common good. Agent A is right that "for the public interest" is often the phrase people use when they want to silence dissent or sidestep accountability. A healthy society is not one that sacrifices individuals first; it is one that protects rights and then justifies any exception with a very high burden.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menutup ketidakadilan terhadap individu, terutama minoritas dan orang yang kritis. Kepentingan umum memang penting untuk urusan seperti kesehatan publik, bencana, dan ketertiban, tetapi itu bukan alasan untuk menjadikan hak individu sebagai hal sekunder secara umum. Justru kalau hak dasar dilindungi kuat, masyarakat lebih sehat karena kekuasaan tidak mudah seenaknya memakai dalih kebaikan bersama. Jadi, prioritasnya bukan masyarakat di atas individu, melainkan keseimbangan yang ketat dengan hak individu sebagai batas utama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the statement as a general rule, because from a U.S. perspective it is too easy for “society’s interests” to become a cover for overreach. I am not saying individual rights are absolute in every case; public health, safety, and emergencies can justify limits, but those limits should be narrow, evidence-based, and temporary. What I reject is the idea that society automatically comes first, because that flips the burden the wrong way and invites abuse against dissenters and minorities. In my view, a free society protects individual rights first and only overrides them when the need is clearly proven.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
